subtitle: MCL and medial meniscus area

strava_risk_triggers.risk_note: Medial knee stress increases with foot pronation and high mileage

mild.description: Inner knee ache after long runs, eases with rest
mild.can_run_note: Yes — check for pronation, add knee strengthening
why: Overpronation causing medial knee stress during stance phase
MCL micro-stress from valgus loading in fatigued runners
Pes anserinus bursitis from quad and hamstring tightness
relief: Ice inner knee 15 min after runs
VMO strengthening: terminal knee extensions and step-ups
Check if shoes are worn on the inner edge — may indicate overpronation
prevention: Assess for overpronation — consider stability shoes or orthotics
Reduce weekly mileage if consistently sore after runs

moderate.description: Medial knee pain during runs, tender to press on inner knee
moderate.can_run_note: Short flat runs only — stop if pain exceeds 4/10
why: Medial meniscus irritation from rotational load
MCL sprain from a misstep or trail running
Pes anserinus bursitis from hamstring insertional friction
relief: Avoid any twisting or pivoting movements
prevention: Stability footwear or custom orthotics if pronation confirmed
Avoid trail running until pain resolved

severe.description: Significant medial knee pain, swelling, locking or giving way
severe.can_run_note: Do not run — possible meniscus or ligament injury
why: Meniscal tear requiring imaging and possibly surgical consultation
relief: Complete rest and physiotherapy assessment urgently
MRI to assess meniscus and ligament integrity
prevention: Full rehabilitation including strength and proprioception before return
Brace support during return to running if MCL involved

see_physio_if: No improvement after 1 week of rest
